docs.title: SmartSepsis-Oph
docs.text:
AI-first molecular diagnostics design for ophthalmic infections.

Phase 0 — computational design only

SmartSepsis-Oph is a research program in computational design phase. All results in this repository are in silico. No claims of clinical performance are made. Experimental validation is pending and is what we are actively seeking collaborators for. This is not a medical device and has not been approved by ANVISA, FDA, or any regulatory body.

Bacterial endophthalmitis can blind an eye in 24–48 hours. Cultures take 48–72. Existing molecular diagnostics are built for sample volumes orders of magnitude larger than what ophthalmology yields (sub-microliter vitreous tap, corneal scrape, ocular swab).

SmartSepsis-Oph attacks this gap with an AI-first design pipeline:

  • Guide-RNA library design (CRISPR-Cas12a) for 12 resistance-gene families relevant to ocular infection, prioritized for the Brazilian epidemiological context.
  • In silico specificity modeling against public reference repositories.
  • Isothermal RPA primer design compatible with thermocycler-free, point-of-care workflows.
  • Functional impact scoring of variants (inspired by Evo 2 / EVEE).
  • Paper-strip assay architecture under design.

First declared target: Staphylococcus aureus / mecA — the most common and best-validated combination in the CRISPR-Dx literature.

docs.title: Status at a glance
docs.text:
Phase Scope Status 0 — Computational design Guide and primer libraries, in silico specificity, scoring, structural analysis. Current 1 — Wet-lab feasibility Single-organism, single-gene validation (S. aureus / mecA). Seeking wet-lab partner 2 — Panel expansion Multiplex of priority resistance markers. Planned 3 — Clinical validation Prospective sampling under IRB. Planned 4 — Regulatory submission IVD (RDC 830/2023) pathway and equivalent. Planned

See the full roadmap.

docs.title: Contributing
docs.text:
The canonical contributing guide lives in the repository root: CONTRIBUTING.md.

This page summarizes the highlights.

docs.title: What we are looking for
docs.text:
1. Wet-lab partners (highest priority) — Cas12a / Cas13 collateral cleavage, isothermal amplification (RPA / LAMP), bacterial culture.
  2. Senior scientific advisors in CRISPR-Dx, ocular microbiology, molecular pathology, isothermal amplification chemistry.
  3. Clinical partners with case volume in endophthalmitis, microbial keratitis, or neonatal ophthalmia.
  4. Funders interested in AI-first diagnostics for neglected sample types.

See Partners for the full version.

docs.title: How to start a conversation
docs.text:
• Email: iaparamedicos@gmail.com.
  • Issue: open one with a partnership, advisor, or funding label.
  • Direct: João Victor Pacheco Dias on LinkedIn.

docs.title: Code contributions
docs.text:
We welcome:

  • Bug fixes and reproducibility improvements.
  • Documentation and tutorial contributions.
  • Independent validation of design pipeline outputs.
  • Additions to the guide-RNA design and scoring modules.

Please open an issue before submitting a substantial PR so we can align on scope.

docs.title: What we ask of contributors
docs.text:
• Honesty about scope. Do not introduce code or documentation that overstates the program's stage or makes performance claims not yet experimentally validated. See Disclaimers.
  • Reproducibility. Provide enough information for someone else to run what you ran. See Reproducibility.
  • Attribution. Cite methods you borrow from published work.

docs.title: Code of conduct
docs.text: Be excellent to each other. A formal Code of Conduct will be published ahead of the framework preprint. Until then, the spirit of the Contributor Covenant applies.

docs.title: Disclaimers
docs.text:
Research use only

SmartSepsis-Oph is a research program by IA para Médicos. The code and designs in this repository are for research use only.

docs.title: No clinical claims
docs.text:
• No claim of clinical performance is made.
  • All results in this repository are in silico.
  • Performance metrics (sensitivity, specificity, limit of detection, time-to-result) will only be reported after experimental validation with a wet-lab partner.

docs.title: Not a medical device
docs.text:
• SmartSepsis-Oph is not a medical device.
  • No medical device approval has been sought or granted under ANVISA, FDA, or any other regulatory body.
  • Do not use any output of this pipeline to make clinical decisions.

docs.title: Regulatory status
docs.text:
• ANVISA (Brazil): no submission. The eventual pathway is anticipated under RDC 830/2023 (IVD).
  • FDA (USA): no submission.
  • CE-IVDR (EU): no submission.

Regulatory submission is a Phase 4 activity on the Roadmap.

docs.title: Not a substitute for standard of care
docs.text:
SmartSepsis-Oph designs do not substitute for:

  • Bacterial culture.
  • Antibiogram.
  • Clonal surveillance.
  • Any other standard-of-care diagnostic.

docs.title: Predicted structures and embeddings
docs.text:
Structures in the published library are predicted (ESMFold / ColabFold AF2 / AlphaFold Server AF3) and carry inherent uncertainty. Use per-variant struct_mean_plddt for weighting.

AlphaFold Server (AF3) terms apply to the mecA1 / mecA2 PDBs — research use only, no commercial redistribution.

docs.title: Conflict of interest
docs.text: Authors declare affiliation with IA para Médicos (project sponsor). No financial conflicts.

docs.title: Contact
docs.text: For any questions about scope or regulatory status, write to iaparamedicos@gmail.com.

docs.title: Published library
docs.text:
The SmartSepsis-Oph multimodal AMR variants dataset is publicly available on Hugging Face:

https://huggingface.co/datasets/JVLegend/smartsepsis-oph

License: CC BY 4.0.

A Zenodo DOI is planned alongside the framework preprint:

Zenodo DOI: to be assigned.

docs.title: Two configs
docs.text: Config Rows Size Contents panel 45 (43 with full pipeline) ~3.2 MB Multimodal: DNA + protein + ESM-2 + ProtT5 + PDB + structural features + drug class. extended 9 034 ~34 MB AMRFinderPlus catalog (8 991) + panel (43): variant_id + source + drug_classes + ESM-2 embedding.

docs.title: Coverage (panel)
docs.text: Family Variants Drug class mecA mecA1, mecA2 penam, cephalosporin, methicillin (MRSA) blaKPC KPC-2, 3, 4, 5, 11, 30, 31 carbapenem, cephalosporin, penam blaNDM NDM-1, 2, 5, 7 carbapenem, cephalosporin, penam blaOXA-48 OXA-48, 181, 232 carbapenem, cephalosporin, penam blaVIM VIM-1, 2, 4 carbapenem, cephalosporin, penam blaIMP IMP-1, 6 carbapenem, cephalosporin, penam blaGES GES-1, 5 carbapenem, cephalosporin blaCTX-M CTX-M-2, 8, 9, 14, 27 cephalosporin, penam (ESBL) vanA vanA glycopeptide (vancomycin) mcr mcr-1, mcr-1.1, mcr-5 polymyxin, peptide qnrS qnrS1, qnrS2 fluoroquinolone armA armA aminoglycoside

docs.title: Schema (panel)
docs.text:
Each row contains:

  • variant_id, gene_family, dna_accession.
  • dna_sequence, protein_sequence, protein_length.
  • drug_classes, resistance_mechanism.
  • esm2_embedding (640d), esm2_model.
  • prott5_embedding (1024d), prott5_model.
  • structure_pdb (full PDB text), structure_source, struct_length.
  • Structural descriptors: struct_rg, struct_compactness, struct_contact_density, struct_mean_plddt.

For the extended config, only variant_id, source, drug_classes, and esm2_embedding are guaranteed.

docs.title: How to load
docs.text:
from datasets import load_dataset
import numpy as np

# Multimodal panel (curated)
ds = load_dataset("JVLegend/smartsepsis-oph", "panel", split="train")
row = ds[0]
emb = np.concatenate([
    np.array(row["esm2_embedding"]),
    np.array(row["prott5_embedding"]),
])  # 1664d ensemble

# Extended (AMRFinderPlus + panel, ESM-2 only)
ds_ext = load_dataset("JVLegend/smartsepsis-oph", "extended", split="train")

docs.title: Construction (summary)
docs.text:
1. Variants pulled from NCBI RefSeq via Entrez, curated against AMRFinderPlus / CARD.
  2. Translation: longest ORF, bacterial table 11.
  3. ESM-2 and ProtT5 mean-pooled embeddings.
  4. 3D structure: ESMFold / ColabFold AF2 / AlphaFold Server AF3 (tiered).
  5. Structure descriptors from Cα coordinates.
  6. Drug class / mechanism from CARD ontology.

See the dataset README on Hugging Face for the full construction notes.

docs.title: License notes
docs.text:
• Data licensed CC BY 4.0.
  • AlphaFold Server (AF3) terms apply to the mecA1 / mecA2 PDBs — research use only, no commercial redistribution. Regenerate via ColabFold AF2 or AlphaFold 2 / OpenFold for commercial use.

docs.title: Reproducibility
docs.text:
Preliminary

Reproducibility documentation is preliminary. The pipeline scripts currently live at the repository root and are being migrated to a packaged src/ layout ahead of the framework preprint. Expect rough edges until that migration lands. See MIGRATION.md in the repository for current status (file may not yet be present on main).

docs.title: Minimal flow
docs.text:
python fetch_sequences.py
python design_guides.py
python covariance_probes.py
python design_primers.py
python specificity_check.py
python run_batch.py 4

docs.title: What is reproducible today
docs.text:
• The published library (data) — fully reproducible from RefSeq accessions + pinned model checkpoints + the construction notes on Hugging Face.
  • Guide and primer designs — deterministic given a reference sequence and the scripts at their pinned commit.
  • In silico specificity — deterministic given a snapshot of the BLAST reference repositories on the pull date recorded in pipeline outputs.

docs.title: What is not yet reproducible
docs.text:
• Performance metrics on real samples — there are none. See Disclaimers.
  • Stress-test specificity — planned, see specificity.

docs.title: Asking for help
docs.text: If you want to reproduce a specific result, open an issue at https://github.com/JVLegend/smartsepsis/issues or email iaparamedicos@gmail.com. We will help.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Pipeline overview.
  • Contributing.

docs.title: Guide-RNA design
docs.text:
Computational design phase

Guide-RNA designs in this repository are in silico candidates. No Cas12a collateral-cleavage validation has been performed. See Roadmap — Phase 1.

docs.title: Approach
docs.text:
Cas12a guide-RNAs are designed by scanning reference target sequences for the canonical TTTV PAM (V = A/C/G) and extracting the downstream spacer. Candidate guides are filtered on:

  • PAM context.
  • Spacer composition (GC range, homopolymer avoidance).
  • Predicted on-target activity.
  • Position within the target gene.

See the source script: design_guides.py.

docs.title: Re-scoring with biophysical features
docs.text:
Candidate guides are then re-scored using 18 biophysical / sequence-context features via covariance_probes.py. The features capture local sequence composition, predicted structure, and proxies for off-target behavior, and are intended as a richer ranking signal than raw on-target score alone.

Details of the feature set are documented under scoring.

docs.title: Target families
docs.text: Initial library covers 12 resistance-gene families relevant to ocular infection (see the published library for the full table). First declared validation target: S. aureus / mecA.

docs.title: Limitations
docs.text:
• All scoring is in silico; predicted activity has not been benchmarked against Cas12a cleavage data on these specific targets.
  • Off-target profiling is BLAST-based — see specificity for caveats and planned stress-tests.

docs.title: Source
docs.text:
• design_guides.py — guide scanning and on-target scoring.
  • covariance_probes.py — 18-feature re-scoring.

docs.title: Multiplex panel
docs.text:
Computational design phase

Panels are designed, not experimentally validated. No multiplex cross-reactivity has been measured.

docs.title: Goal
docs.text: Combine guide-RNA / primer designs across the priority resistance-gene families into a single point-of-care panel compatible with the paper-strip assay architecture under design.

docs.title: Optimization
docs.text:
multiplex_panel.py selects a subset of guides and primers that:

  • Cover the prioritized clinical phenotypes (see library).
  • Minimize predicted cross-reactivity within the panel.
  • Respect amplicon-size and reaction-compatibility constraints for RPA.
  • Balance per-gene representation against panel size.

docs.title: Clinical interpretation
docs.text: clinical_interpreter.py produces a natural-language summary of a panel result mapping detected guides to drug class and resistance mechanism via the CARD enrichment layer.

docs.title: Future work
docs.text:
• Cross-reactivity mapping at the wet-lab stage — see Roadmap — Phase 2.
  • Paper-strip / lateral flow prototype.

docs.title: Sources
docs.text:
• multiplex_panel.py — panel optimization.
  • clinical_interpreter.py — natural-language interpretation.

docs.title: Pipeline overview
docs.text:
Computational design phase

The pipeline described below is entirely in silico. No experimental validation has been performed. Outputs are research artifacts intended to inform downstream wet-lab work.

The SmartSepsis-Oph design pipeline has seven stages. Each stage is implemented as a script at the repository root (migration to a packaged src/ layout is planned — see Roadmap).

docs.title: Flow
docs.text:
flowchart TD
    A[1. Fetch sequences<br/>fetch_sequences.py] --> B[2. Guide design<br/>design_guides.py]
    B --> C[3. Covariance probes<br/>covariance_probes.py]
    C --> D[4. Primer design<br/>design_primers.py]
    D --> E[5. BLAST specificity<br/>specificity_check.py]
    E --> F[6. Scoring + CARD<br/>evo2_scoring.py + card_integration.py]
    F --> G[7. Clinical interpretation<br/>+ multiplex panel<br/>clinical_interpreter.py + multiplex_panel.py]
    G --> H[(Published artifacts:<br/>JSON / CSV / HF dataset)]

docs.title: 1. Fetch sequences
docs.text: Reference DNA sequences for the 12 resistance-gene families are pulled from NCBI RefSeq via Entrez. Accessions are curated against AMRFinderPlus and CARD ontology. See data library.

docs.title: 2. Guide design
docs.text: CRISPR-Cas12a guide-RNAs scanning for the TTTV PAM across reference targets, with on-target scoring and PAM-context filtering. See guide design.

docs.title: 3. Covariance probes
docs.text: Re-scoring of candidate guides with 18 biophysical features capturing sequence context, structure, and predicted on-/off-target behavior. See scoring.

docs.title: 4. Primer design
docs.text: Isothermal RPA primer pairs (37 °C operating point), tuned for thermocycler-free, point-of-care workflows.

docs.title: 5. BLAST specificity
docs.text: In silico specificity assessment against public reference repositories. A planned stress-test pass against close non-target genomes will further probe selectivity. See specificity.

docs.title: 6. Scoring + CARD
docs.text: Functional variant impact scoring (Evo 2 / EVEE inspired) plus enrichment from the CARD database for drug class, resistance mechanism, and ontology.

docs.title: 7. Clinical interpretation + multiplex
docs.text: Natural-language interpretation of designs and multiplex panel optimization for downstream paper-strip assay architecture. See multiplex.

docs.title: Companion modules
docs.text: • Structural analysis (ESM-2, ProtT5, AlphaFold) feeds variant-level features into scoring and into the published dataset. See structural analysis.

docs.title: Reproducibility
docs.text: See Data → Reproducibility for the current state of reproducibility documentation.

docs.title: Scoring
docs.text:
Computational design phase

Scores are predictive features, not measured activities.

docs.title: Functional variant scoring
docs.text:
Variant-level functional impact is scored with an approach inspired by Evo 2 / EVEE: large-scale sequence likelihood under a genomic language model is used as a proxy for the fitness effect of a variant relative to wild type.

See evo2_scoring.py.

docs.title: 18 biophysical features (covariance probes)
docs.text:
Candidate guides are re-scored against 18 biophysical and sequence-context features capturing:

  • Local sequence composition (GC, dinucleotide bias).
  • Predicted secondary structure context around the spacer.
  • Position-weighted nucleotide identity.
  • Proxies for off-target propensity.

These features are computed in covariance_probes.py and used to rank candidates from guide design.

docs.title: CARD enrichment
docs.text:
Scored variants are cross-referenced against the CARD ontology (card_integration.py) for:

  • Drug class.
  • Resistance mechanism.
  • Ontology lineage.

This feeds the clinical interpretation stage and the published library.

docs.title: Caveats
docs.text:
• Sequence-likelihood scores are proxies, not measured fitness.
  • The 18-feature panel was built to rank within a candidate pool, not to give calibrated absolute activity.

docs.title: Sources
docs.text:
• evo2_scoring.py — functional variant scoring.
  • covariance_probes.py — 18-feature re-scoring.
  • card_integration.py — CARD ontology enrichment.

docs.title: Specificity
docs.text:
Computational design phase

Specificity here means in silico specificity. Experimental cross-reactivity has not been measured.

docs.title: In silico approach
docs.text: Candidate guides and primer pairs are screened against public reference repositories using BLAST (specificity_check.py). Matches are filtered by identity, coverage, and taxonomy to flag potential off-target hits.

docs.title: Planned stress-test
docs.text:
A planned stress-test pass will probe selectivity against:

  • Close non-target genomes within the same genus.
  • Common ocular surface commensals.
  • Phylogenetically adjacent resistance gene variants.

Details will live at scripts/README_stress_test.md once the stress-test suite lands.1

docs.title: Caveats
docs.text:
• BLAST-based specificity is a necessary but not sufficient check. Wet-lab cross-reactivity testing is required before any performance claim.
  • The reference repositories evolve; specificity calls are valid as of the pull date recorded in pipeline outputs.

docs.title: Source
docs.text:
• specificity_check.py — BLAST orchestration and filtering.

  1. At the time of writing, scripts/README_stress_test.md is being prepared in a sibling work-stream and may not yet be present on main. ↩

docs.title: Structural analysis
docs.text:
Computational design phase

Structures are predicted, not experimentally determined. Predicted structures carry inherent uncertainty (see pLDDT).

docs.title: Embeddings
docs.text:
Each variant in the published library is annotated with two protein-language-model embeddings:

  • ESM-2 (esm2_t30_150M_UR50D) — 640-dim, mean-pooled across residues.
  • ProtT5 (Rostlab/prot_t5_xl_uniref50) — 1024-dim, mean-pooled.

Concatenated, this gives a 1664-dim multimodal protein representation usable as a downstream feature for classifiers and similarity search.

docs.title: 3D structure prediction
docs.text:
Per-variant 3D structures are predicted using a tiered approach:

Method Used for ESMFold (HuggingFace transformers) Proteins ≤ 400 aa. ColabFold AF2 Mid-size targets (e.g. mcr-1, mcr-5 ~540 aa). AlphaFold Server (AF3) Larger targets (mecA1, mecA2 ~665 aa).

rank_1 is selected per variant.

docs.title: Structure descriptors
docs.text:
From the predicted Cα coordinates we compute:

  • Radius of gyration (struct_rg).
  • Compactness ratio Rg / L^0.6 (struct_compactness).
  • Contact density (fraction of Cα–Cα pairs < 8 Å).
  • Mean Cα–Cα distance.
  • Aspect ratio.
  • Mean pLDDT — prediction confidence (0–100). Use this for downstream weighting; local regions can be substantially lower than the mean.

docs.title: Caveats
docs.text: • AlphaFold Server (AF3) terms apply to the mecA1 / mecA2 PDBs — research use only, no commercial redistribution. For commercial use, regenerate via ColabFold AF2 or AlphaFold 2 / OpenFold.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Published library — schema and access.
  • Scoring — how structural features feed ranking.

docs.title: What this project is
docs.text:
SmartSepsis-Oph is an AI-first computational design program for CRISPR-based point-of-care diagnostics targeting ophthalmic infections — endophthalmitis, microbial keratitis, and ocular surface infections.

The pipeline produces:

  • CRISPR-Cas12a guide-RNA libraries for 12 resistance-gene families.
  • Isothermal RPA primer designs compatible with thermocycler-free workflows.
  • In silico specificity analyses against public reference repositories.
  • Functional variant impact scoring (inspired by Evo 2 / EVEE).
  • A paper-strip assay architecture under design.

First declared target: S. aureus / mecA.

docs.title: What this project is not
docs.text:
• Not a medical device.
  • Not validated experimentally — every metric in this repository is in silico.
  • Not a substitute for culture, antibiogram, or clonal surveillance.
  • Not approved for any clinical use under ANVISA, FDA, or any other regulatory body.

Performance metrics (sensitivity, specificity, limit of detection, time-to-result) will only be reported after experimental validation with a wet-lab partner.

docs.title: The ophthalmology gap
docs.text:
Existing molecular diagnostics are built for sample volumes orders of magnitude larger than what ophthalmology yields:

  • Vitreous tap: sub-microliter.
  • Corneal scrape / ocular swab: trace amounts of nucleic acid.

Cultures take 48–72 hours; endophthalmitis blinds in 24–48. The diagnostic window is incompatible with the standard of care.

docs.title: Why AI-first
docs.text: We use protein language models (ESM-2, ProtT5), structural prediction (ESMFold / ColabFold / AlphaFold), and functional variant scoring to design guide-RNA and primer libraries that are evaluated computationally before any wet-lab cycle is spent. This is the only realistic way to compress the design-validation loop for a sample-scarce, time-critical indication.

docs.title: See also
docs.text:
• Roadmap — phased plan.
  • Team — who is involved.
  • Partners — what we are seeking.
  • Methods — the seven-step pipeline.
  • Disclaimers — regulatory and research-use framing.

docs.title: Partners we are seeking
docs.text:
SmartSepsis-Oph is in Phase 0 (computational design). To move to Phase 1 we need partners across four categories.

Co-authorship offer

Co-authorship on the forthcoming framework preprint is offered to early collaborators across all four categories below.

docs.title: 1. Wet-lab partners (highest priority)
docs.text:
Laboratory capability in:

  • CRISPR-Dx (Cas12a / Cas13 collateral cleavage assays).
  • Isothermal amplification (RPA, LAMP).
  • Bacterial culture and DNA extraction at standard biosafety levels.
  • Optional: paper-strip / lateral flow prototyping.

In exchange we offer:

  • Joint design of the experimental plan, starting with S. aureus / mecA.
  • Co-authorship on the framework preprint and on subsequent papers.
  • Open data sharing and reproducible analysis.
  • Visibility in the project's public communication.

docs.title: 2. Senior scientific advisors
docs.text: See Team — open advisor slots.

docs.title: 3. Clinical partners
docs.text:
Hospitals or eye centers with case volume in:

  • Post-cataract endophthalmitis.
  • Post-injection (anti-VEGF) endophthalmitis.
  • Microbial keratitis (especially contact-lens related).
  • Neonatal ophthalmia.

For future prospective sampling under IRB.

docs.title: 4. Funders
docs.text: Seed-stage funders interested in AI-first diagnostics for neglected sample types. We are open to grants, philanthropic capital, and aligned strategic investment.

docs.title: How to start a conversation
docs.text:
1. Email: iaparamedicos@gmail.com with a brief note about your interest and what you can bring.
  2. Issue: open an issue on GitHub with the partnership, advisor, or funding label.
  3. Direct: reach out to João Victor Pacheco Dias on LinkedIn.

See also Contributing.

docs.title: Roadmap
docs.text:
Honest framing

Status badges below reflect what has actually been delivered. We do not claim progress in phases we have not yet entered.

docs.title: Phase 0 — Computational design
docs.text:
Scope. Guide and primer libraries, in silico specificity, functional scoring, structural analysis.

Deliverables:

  • CRISPR-Cas12a guide-RNA design pipeline (guide design).
  • Covariance probe re-scoring with 18 biophysical features (scoring).
  • Isothermal RPA primer design.
  • In silico specificity check via BLAST (specificity).
  • CARD database enrichment.
  • Published multimodal AMR dataset on Hugging Face (library).
  • Framework preprint.
  • Migration of pipeline scripts under a packaged src/ layout.

docs.title: Phase 1 — Wet-lab feasibility
docs.text:
Scope. Single-organism, single-gene validation (S. aureus / mecA) in cultured isolates.

Deliverables (planned):

  • Cas12a collateral-cleavage validation on synthetic targets.
  • RPA amplification check on bacterial genomic DNA.
  • Specificity stress-test against close non-target genomes.
  • Limit-of-detection envelope (preliminary).

docs.title: Phase 2 — Panel expansion
docs.text:
Scope. Multiplex of priority resistance markers and real-amplicon validation.

  • Multiplex panel optimization (multiplex).
  • Cross-reactivity mapping inside the panel.
  • Paper-strip / lateral flow prototype.

docs.title: Phase 3 — Clinical validation
docs.text:
Scope. Prospective sampling under IRB; head-to-head against culture and PCR.

  • IRB protocol with clinical partner.
  • Sample-handling SOPs for sub-microliter ocular fluids.
  • Prospective performance evaluation.

docs.title: Phase 4 — Regulatory submission
docs.text:
Scope. IVD pathway (ANVISA RDC 830/2023) and equivalent.

  • Quality management system.
  • Analytical and clinical performance dossier.
  • Regulatory submission.

docs.title: How to move us forward
docs.text: If you can help bridge Phase 0 → Phase 1, see Partners or write to iaparamedicos@gmail.com.

  • Contact: iaparamedicos@gmail.com

docs.title: Dr. Gustavo Sakuno — Clinical advisor
docs.text:

  • PhD, USP — Ophthalmology & Oculomics.
  • Clinical and scientific lead for the ophthalmology axis.

docs.title: Open advisor slots
docs.text:
We are actively seeking senior scientific advisors in:

  • CRISPR-based diagnostics — SHERLOCK, DETECTR, and successor platforms.
  • Ocular microbiology and ophthalmic infectious disease.
  • Molecular pathology and clinical diagnostic regulation.
  • Isothermal amplification chemistry (RPA, LAMP).

Advisor engagement can be light-touch (named advisor + occasional review) or deeper. We will not ask any advisor to endorse claims we have not yet substantiated — see Disclaimers.

If you are interested, see Partners or email iaparamedicos@gmail.com.

docs.title: Affiliation
docs.text: SmartSepsis-Oph is a research program by IA para Médicos.
